> a group of young men was examined during 1-h head-out immersions in water of different temperatures (32°C, 20°C and 14°C). Cold water immersion (14°C) lowered rectal temperature and increased metabolic rate (by 350%), heart rate and systolic and diastolic blood pressure (by 5%, 7%, and 8%, respectively). **Plasma noradrenaline and dopamine concentrations were increased by 530% and by 250% respectively,** while diuresis increased by 163% (more than at 32°C). Plasma aldosterone concentrations increased by 23%. Plasma renin activity was reduced as during immersion in water at the highest temperature. Cortisol concentrations tended to decrease.

That's one of my favorite papers Sean. I also like the 350% metabolic increase with only a heart rate 5% and BP 7-8% increase. I think about how some poor dude is pounding pavement or straining away in a gym to get that metabolic rate while really pushing the HR and BP high while I'm sitting in my cold tub like a lazy bookworm reading one of my food history books. Embracing the cold and engaging the uncoupled features is like realizing what you thought was a moped you were on is actually a MotoGT bike and you're at the starting line.

> [Ashley said:](/goto/post?id=230263)
>
> I know it's hard to quantify, but I wonder how long it may take for me to feel happy/excitable/motivated towards life again. Not in a "woe is me" way, but more of a neurotransmitter way. Anyone have any guestimates on how long it may take for a dopamine depleted person to replenish?

You know, more and more I see UK members posting here and I have to say that I appreciate the efforts they must go through to master the American language. Bravo UK!

Ashley, you're a youngster. At least I work with a 60 year old that tells me that so I have to spread the micro-aggression around... Different person and different issue, but I noticed that it took my wife longer than me to see benefits. My wife's sleep improved and her weight totally stabilized after about 5 weeks of 3x per week CT body immersion 55-58F water. She can still totally demolish her sleep with bad eating or light hygiene at night, so keep those factors in mind too.

# Thread: Introduction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction.20430/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11

### Donnyooh — 2017-11-12T02:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction.20430/post-227813>

Hi all
I'm a mature student (MA in Cognitive Behavioural Therapy) finally getting to pursue a career as a psychotherapist after 20 years working as an engineering technician.
I'm married and a father of two living in Dublin ,Ireland
My daughter aged 6 carries a very large benign ganglioneuroma tumor which has robbed her of her mobility and bowel control and if it wasn't for her I wouldn't have taken any interest in anything related to mitochondria and lower heteroplasmy rates as the cause of neolithic disease.

I began my journey to educate myself around alternative solutions that might be out there for my daughters condition.I 'm currently really interested in the possibilities of focussed ultrasound as an alternative to surgery for my daughters tumor ,which is a positive application of microwaves.
My son aged 9 has a language disorder and is possibly on the autism spectrum.He exhibits some traits but overall its fairly mild but I was also interested in anything I could learn from Dr Jack kruse that could help him .
My initial journey began really in looking at ways to reduce inflammation which led me to investigate the Low Carb diet movement mainly by following a Banting diet (Professor Tim Noakes,Real Meal Revolution).However,I came across Dr Jack Kruse at a time when I was really doubting that it was purely about reducing carbs and insulin resistance.
I felt instinctively that there were pieces missing in the arguments.

So far my changes at home have been.....
Walking with kids to school to increase exposure to AM sunlight.
Reduction in blue light exposure in the evenings
Reverse osmosis filtering of the drinking water to remove flouride mainly.
Switching off the WiFi at home and going ethernet.
Maintaining a low carb diet plan as much as possible (hard with kids)

Looking forward to learning a lot from you guys !

# Thread: Dave’s Hashimoto’s and myopia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dave%E2%80%99s-hashimoto%E2%80%99s-and-myopia.20435/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### David Moorefield — 2017-11-13T19:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dave%E2%80%99s-hashimoto%E2%80%99s-and-myopia.20435/post-227915>

I’m 43, IT worker near Washington DC

Sub-clinical for Hashimoto’s HypoThyroidism: Diagnosed 2013 after moving to the high EMF Northern Virginia area in 2009. I cut out gluten and most grains and the TPO antibodies have been dropping mostly steadily. I rediscovered and added a lot more sun starting about summer of 2016 and then the TSH went normal and TPO is almost normal. My TPO is does seem lower in the fall after the summer and higher after the winter. I now block my thyroid and eyes when I can in front of blue/poor quality light.

I was positive for Epstein Barr Virus (associated with Hashimoto’s) and have used a regime of Olive Leaf Extract, Colliodal Silver, Oregano Oil, BHT, 10G vitC per day and sun for over a year now I’m negative for EBV Nuclear Antigen Ab, IgG.

Myopia (nearsightedness) since 4th grade: -6 diopters now, fits with possible progressive blue light toxicity. I’ve started to do some ‘print-pushing’ (plus sun/red/NIR of course) to being to adapt my eyes back to a lower diopter since my -6 may cause tearing etc as I age.

Per Jack interview I try to be up for the first hour of sun and stay when the UVa begins for another hour. I try to pop out frequently for morning sun and vitD when available. I’ve slowly started doing some CT as well per probable maternal haplotype. Indoors I use a lizard light, a UV bulb, Joovv red/NIR lights morning and night.

Per Jack I am taking some small trips away from DC to give mitochondria a break from EMF, 5G etc.

Per jack I got my 23andMe information with maternal haplotype to get a clearer indication of how to treat the Hashimoto’s and underlying issues. I guess confirming my German mitochondria haplotype indicate a need to do plenty of Cold Therapy? With my dietary restrictions and light environment improved it seems the Hashimoto’s is close to being resolved?...

data:
My maternal haplotype: J2a1a, Paternal haplotype:: R-P311
I'm currently living in Arlington, VA latitude 38.8 degrees
My blood type:B

I'm heterozygous for MTHFR C677T and MTHFR A1298C and VDR and CBS
SNPs breakdown:
MTHFR C677T rs1801133 GG -/-
MTHFR A1298C rs1801131 TT -/-
VDR Bsm rs1544410 CT +/-
VDR Taq rs731236 AG +/-
CBS C699T rs234706 GG -/-

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### David Moorefield — 2017-11-14T04:51:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dave%E2%80%99s-hashimoto%E2%80%99s-and-myopia.20435/post-227960>

25-Hydroxy Vitamin D is 90 ng/mL

When I was originally diagnosed we did a full T/3/T4 workup and it was normal, and since my TSH is much better now, my antibodies much lower and I never really experienced noticeable HypoThyroid issues I don't see a reason to recheck now.

What people don't always mention is that Virtigo is linked. I had a sudden case of Virtigo before my Hashimoto diagnosis, so my body was attacking that part of my body as well. It was out of the blue, nothing like it before or after.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T05:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dave%E2%80%99s-hashimoto%E2%80%99s-and-myopia.20435/post-227962>

You said, "I’m 43, IT worker near Washington DC."

**My Rx:

The knack of a good life lies in learning how to take off a runway in your mind and miss the ground because you're piloting an internal flight. The secret of this kind of flight is this -- you have to do it immediately and often, before your body realizes it is defying the laws of nature. Every person has inside himself a parasitic being who is acting not at all to their advantage in some way. It is your job to find out what this being in you is doing to you. You must go there to tend to that being.

Peace is an internal settlement of an eternal conflict within. One one side sits intuition and nature and on the other is beliefs and perspectives. The flights we must take within require a scrub brush to clear debris of what was built by yesterday's beliefs. It is a chronic battle to fight what you were before because those lesson seem so much more important to existence than the the new one's you get everyday. This is false. What was important yesterday was for a time in the past that no longer exists for you today, you just do not realize it but your disease manifest because of it. Knowing this about yourself is the beginning of the application of all the wisdom nature can bring to bare on you. You musn't lie to your soul, but strive to live within the question when the answers you are given are no good to you. The deeper you go into yourself in these mito-hacking flights, the more you'll realize that you are your own worse enemy. This is when regeneration commences.
**
_I hope you take this in the right light.

It is said with great intention._

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-15T05:27:57+0200

### mmarston — 2017-11-17T18:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dave%E2%80%99s-hashimoto%E2%80%99s-and-myopia.20435/post-228283>

Like your graph, David. I've had elevated thyroid antibodies for awhile now (+7 years). Last year I eliminated gluten and dairy from my diet as best I could and did not see my antibodies nor TSH drop, however my spring allergies to pollen were greatly improved.

This fall, I started taking 200 mg of selenium and went totally grain-free about 2-3 weeks before my annual health checkup to see if that would reduce my thyroid antibodies. Here are the results:

**Oct 2017 (Oct 2016)**
Tg <20 (398)
TPO 176 (81)
TSH 3.06 (2.22)
T4, free 1.00 (1.02)
T3, free 2.6 (2.5)
RT3 13.5 (12)

In short, my Tg antibodies were reduced to NON-DETECTABLE  possibly by going grain-free and adding selenium?? My Dr was quite thrilled and surprised. Still a mystery though is why my TPO and TSH went up? Apparently I still have some detective work to see what's triggering the TPO antibodies. I think my new job has something to do with it...since this August started working in a new office with no windows and lots of computers, cell phones, blue light. I do reduce blue light on my screens (f.lux) and go outside as much as I can. Also, I'm currently following Izabella Wentz's '90-day Hashimoto's Protocol' and will remove some other food triggers (soy, eggs, nightshades, nuts/seeds).

For you, maybe just going totally grain-free, healing the leaky gut and experimenting with BOTH Dr Wentz's and Dr Kruse's suggestions may help get your antibodies drop to ND. Good luck!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dave%E2%80%99s-hashimoto%E2%80%99s-and-myopia.20435/post-228554>

mmarston, thanks for your suggestions... Wentz seems understand more about root causes than others and I've really come to enjoy being in the sun... despite my family freaking out ;-)

congratulations on your ND... I think being grain-free for at least 6 months may help not poor fuel on the fire (per Dr Osborne 70% of people need to be grain-free), but the sun and light practices seem to strike to the root cause of the problem which is what we want...

_Think about it. What does CT or cold/ice baths do? Decreases inflammation, reduces the size/length of the ECT (electron chain transport), makes your electron flow more efficient, increases magnetism in the cells, builds a bigger EZ which keeps out toxins/molds/virus and makes the body have a bigger battery, the lowers your heteroplasty rate, reduces the amount of energy your body needs to fix/replace defective mitochondria, reduces the amount of nnEMF your body and muscles are being exposed to while in the water.

So Bill Murray does not recommend ice baths after a workout because of ...why? Maybe he thinks inflammation which is created during a workout is good for you because if you increase the inflammation state of the muscles and/or the time you/your muscles stay inflamed, you will build bigger muscles because more HDO and D2O is collected in your muscles. BTW: Who is Bill Murray?_

### Hbuttel — 2018-02-04T01:30:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-ct-right-after-training-limit-the-effect-of-a-workout.20436/post-234063>

Studies have shown that Ct within one hour after weight lifting reduces strength and muscle building.
It is the opposite for endurance training.
After one hour seems to be beneficial for muscle building and strength training

It was talked about in joe rogans podcast with. Ben greenfield, and also Rhonda Patrick's on
cold thermogenesis.

It has something to do with the inflammation being beneficial post workout for a little while

[https://itunes.apple.com/us/podcast/foundmyfitness/id818198322?mt=2&i=1000362813680](https://itunes.apple.com/us/podcast/foundmyfitness/id818198322?mt=2&i=1000362813680)

Mercola also mentions it here
\-
[https://www.google.com/amp/s/fitnes...hive/2017/11/17/amp/cryotherapy-benefits.aspx](https://www.google.com/amp/s/fitness.mercola.com/sites/fitness/archive/2017/11/17/amp/cryotherapy-benefits.aspx)

"There is one important caveat worth mentioning. When you’re doing strength training, the oxidative stress generates reactive oxygen species (ROS) that actually help increase muscle mass. If you expose yourself to cold within the first hour after strength training, you suppress that beneficial process, so avoid doing cold immersion (such as a really cold shower or ice bath) immediately after strength training.

On the other hand, spending some time in the sauna after exercise may actually help increase muscle mass. It’ll also help with detoxification, allowing you to sweat out toxins that can wreak havoc on mitochondrial function in general. As explained by [Rhonda Patrick](https://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/05/01/health-benefits-extreme-hot-cold-temperatures.aspx), Ph.D., in a previous interview:

_“This is what’s important to understand: Exercise is a stress on the body. You’re making ROS. You’re generating inflammation. But that’s a good thing because it’s a short burst, and you want it … There’s a one hour timeframe from the time you stop exercising \[in which inflammation peaks\]._

_That is the stressful period. But then as soon as an hour hits, the stress response kicks in and you start to have a potent anti-inflammatory \[response\]. You start having an antioxidant response from activating all these good genes that stay activate for a long time._

_What happens is that because the cold also is causing an anti-inflammatory response, it’s important that you don’t get that anti-inflammatory response too soon, because you need some of that exercise-induced inflammation. You want that inflammation to happen to get the anti-inflammatory response. That’s important for the strength training._

_The inflammation you generate during the strength training is part of the mechanism for making more proteins in the skeletal muscle. If you blunt that, then you’re going to blunt the effects of the strength training. The question is then can you do it an hour or two hours later? Studies have shown, yes, you can do cold exposure, cold water immersion and actually get some performance enhancements even from doing \[that\].”_
​

# Thread: Jack's Journal into the deep and dark.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T02:41:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227943>

11/12/2017.

You want to know what makes me tick? This does. Every word and image married to the words of this iconic song. It is what drives me everyday. My favorite all time song and video. “When I was a little kid,' I said, sitting down, 'the wallpaper in my room had pictures of Noah's story.'
You know what's weird though? It's weird that the ark would be such a kids' story, you know? I mean, it's...really a story about death and extinction. Every person who isn't in Noah's family? They just die. Every animal, apart from two of each on the boat? They just are wiped out. They all die in the flood. Billions of creatures just vanish. It's the worst tragedy ever,' I finished, my voice tied off by a knot in my chest with this realization and I said in my inner voice 'What the hell was that doing on my wallpaper?” Then I put on a record below in 1977 and realized why it was on my wallpaper. I will have more to say about that topic tomorrow on my Dr. Jack Kruse FB wall.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T02:52:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227945>

I enjoy black swans. I build arks for them. The **black swan theory** of events is a [metaphor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metaphor) that describes an event that comes as a surprise to a paradigm of belief, has a major effect on those who realize it, and is often inappropriately rationalized by the paradigm after the fact with the benefit of [hindsight](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindsight). The term is based on an ancient saying which presumed black swans did not exist, but the saying was rewritten after [black swans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_swan) were discovered in the wild. The implication is, absence of evidence is not absence of effect or reality or truth. Just because evidence is not present right here, right now, does not mean the possibility cannot exist or is not true. What nature allows, will certainly happen. It is the basis of the Kruse Thereom of living systems. This is why the wise among us build arks for the black swans among us. As they gain wisdom in unison, they begin to flock together. Then, the ark of wisdom, buried deep within the threads of nature can protect them from the poor thinking and the beliefs of paradigms built on half truths.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T02:56:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227946>

**KRUSE’S THEOREM:** Any significant technological advancement that man can design or invent has already been incorporated into living systems by evolutionary biology in some way. You just have to look for it. In order for the biological and medical sciences to advance, its practitioners must realize that mother nature is not only smarter and faster than they are but also smarter and faster than our best physicists and engineers have ever been. And they are all painting on the same canvas, but none of them realize it.

wow .....it's taken you five years to start a journal!

Welcome!

Black Swan's should just got to 8:50 of this video and let their hair down til 14:00 Why? Cause some of us black swans are still alive. That is why we build arks for black swans. As long as the smallest things in you move there is still time to change the road you're on.

As long as we keep moving forward.....

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T03:49:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227956>

Life is the King of pain. Black swan's embrace this aspect of life because it usually represents a little black spot on the sun.

This iconic system of thought
a union of man and meaning
What is seeming to happen
when we make symbols out of thought?

Icons are everywhere if we pay attention.
They give meaning to meaning,
the icons of our dreams,
hungry for the next muse,
hyponotized by distractions.

Trapped in this human skin, we create mazes of perceptions,
and hallucinate what makes us great.

Iconic images are wired into our consciousness and like
phantom limbs can hurt us if we get lazy and stop
reinventing ourselves.

Isn't it iconic how ironic can be in this modern world?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T05:37:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227965>

I'm here to remind you.

Y'all ought to know by now.

I'm not quite as well, and I thought you should know.

We've got to slow the light down properly to get what we all want.

Time. Health span. Longevity.

So for those of you who heard my Q & A last night on the November 2017 webinar you heard what I called the surface of the sun and our tissues. Now I will extend it here in my own journal. What if I told you you are made of a special type of plasma that can slow light down to zero and many speeds because of the bio-molecules you have. When you ask me what life is, fundamanetally from this perspective, I tell you since tissues are capable of stopping star light dead in its tracks. Nature job is to send photons 93 million miles away into a condensed matter state, where star light is capable of imprinting its information onto atoms of water that can be magnetically and electrically confined onto the surface topology of nucleic acids to create a quantum evolution????? Would you believe me? Most would say I've lost my marbles when they contemplate this idea, but when you go back and read the old blogs in the Energy and Epigenetic series you see the idea is buried in those blogs. The epigenetic software program of life is built using starlight and water.

So, let me re-introduce you to KRUSE’S THEOREM WHICH STATES: Any significant technological advancement that man can design or invent has already been incorporated into living systems by evolutionary biology in some way. You just have to look for it to see if nature will allow it to happen. If it breaks no natural laws you can bet nature uses it in creating a living system.

In order for the biological and medical sciences to advance, its practitioners must realize that mother nature is not only smarter and faster than they are, but also smarter and faster than our best physicists and engineers have ever been in all of humanity. Those wise engineers have no idea they are all painting on the same canvas. Is there any proof that my theory of a quantum evolution using light and a Bose-Einstein condenstate is spot on in modern physics? Let me introduce you to Lene Hau at Harvard. [http://physicstoday.scitation.org/d...al&utm\_source=twitter.com&utm\_campaign=buffer](http://physicstoday.scitation.org/do/10.1063/PT.6.6.20171113a/full/?utm_content=buffer52f18&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter.com&utm_campaign=buffer)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T05:46:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227966>

To assess the effects of expanded hydration Pollack et al. measured Brownian displacements of microspheres exposed to light of varying intensities and wavelengths. Displacements were consistently diminished in an intensity-dependent and wavelength-dependent fashion, and center-to-center distances between microspheres were shifted to higher values. We conclude that suspended microspheres are surrounded by hydration zones substantial enough to impact Brownian displacements. This idea shows why mitochondrial position in cells are important and where verified in Meghan McMannus's work on the IMJ's of a colony of mitochondria. The colony of mitochondria all line up and oscillate in the same fashion their ability to make energy rises in a non linear fashion. So the question arises for the mitochondriac, is there a way to increase this tune in mitochondria in some way in a mito-hack??? Answer: Yes. Our colonies of mitochondria seem to be tunable to light. Is there data out there in condensed matter physics that show us this is plausible? Yes. This is a key clue to the quantum clinician how to help a person with elevated heteroplasmy rates in a tissue line that we can use light to photo-tune them back to health by caputring and slowing light down. This is one of the down stream effects of understanding how to use light in the coming age of quantum biology. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22739052](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22739052)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-14T15:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-227977>

How do I think about health? Prevention of disease is always the choice over treatment of any disease. Talking prevention is not popular in my world because there is no money in prevention in the healthcare paradigm. Prevention is like looking at a distant rainbow and deciding you need to be prepared for serious storm few think are coming. Knowledge, in this sense, is like an ark to keep you well no matter the storms headed your way. Anytime I see a rainbow now, what comes into my mind is how skillful and talented someone was to create an ark that didn't leak through a prolong period of flood. We must work our talents out and work them out skillfully and then our rainbow of excellence will show the voyeurs your value. So why do you come here everyday and look what I have to say??? You know I'm trying to navigate the storms of disease headed your way in this modern world we've built. **_Remember, it wasn't raining when Noah built his Ark either._** Maybe that is why you congregate here every AM?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-23T06:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-228697>

Have you ever wondered by sea turtles and salmon know exactly where to return to give birth? It is one of the most amazing coherent aspects of biology. Cooperativity has been a fundamental concept in our understanding of biological systems for over a century. But we have act like we have no idea where this biologic hysteresis lies......or what the symptoms are when we lose it.

We know.

It appears Mother Nature was inspired by a model of ferromagnetism; this hysteresis occurs as a consequence of cooperative binding in the limit of large complexes under the power of UV and IR light in the electrons and protons of bio-molecules. Light is the magnetic glue of life. It is commands water to hold that double helix intact and when water loses its firm grip.......we see ferritin in the plasma. Ferritin is like blood in the ocean after a shark attack. Science can now provide us a method to relate the amount of hysteresis to the strength of the neighboring interactions between bound proteins and DNA. This novel kinetic feature of macromolecular complexes surrounded by water allows cells to create a binary response to small changes in external conditions and causes complexes to retain a memory of past conditions over long timescales and that hystesis is stored in water networks just like magnetic domains is stored in rocks on our surface once they cool from their molten state. Sea turtles and salmon use those compases to navigate home.
Are there proteins and atoms in our cells that become magnetized by parts of the solar spectrum when we are born?

Short answer is yes. It turns out UV light magnetizes bases in our RNA and DNA. That is the memory that remains in us all. The cell is a play room for photons and photons have both electric and magnetic properties that does something wizard like to the plasma inside cells. Life is based upon the entangled activity of photobiochemical and photobiophysical processes. Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolic chaos.

Atomic mass was a big topic in the Time 2 blog. This is why my Patreon series on deuterium is quite a big deal. Quite surprisingly when you look at the atomic diameters of O and H atoms are relatively similar despite their difference in atomic mass. When atomic diameters match, this allows for light frequencies to also match these atoms, as a controlling lever of the reactants via a molecular resonance phenomena. When allowing for this frequency matching, it appears O and H can be controlled by light frequencies in the visible range near room temperature. Oxygen atoms are driven through repeating heat (IR/red) fluctuations by cyclical movement through the human body, frequency matching with hydrogen atoms that are made with a spin of 1/2.

The resonant frequency of hydrogen (H1) in its rest state is 3,773,180 Hz, according to the element's atomic diameter at 20°C. Oxygen isotope (O16) resonates at this same frequency when heated to 37.8°C.

All nucleic acids fluoresce at 340-350nm well within the UV range. Ironic that medicine thinks UV light is toxic, when our most sensitive protein craves those specific frequencies to magnetize our blueprint of life don't ya think???? That means their incident light that is absorbed from the environment has to be below this emission spectra frequency. That is hard core UVB range light below 340nm; probably 260nm-330nm. EZ water has a specific peak at 270nm with a massive heat capacity for IR from 600-3100nm. Coincidence or quantum design?

I think we can store 260 nm and above because base pairs have unique abilities in a stacked helix. DNA and RNA become a molecular exciton acting like a Birkland electric current with a specific pinch. These repeitive pinchs and helices delays photon emission and stores light naturally just like a star does in its condensed matter. Macrocosm meets microcosm...........in physics and biologic organization

The more UV light you store the more compact your DNA and RNA become limiting epigenetic activity and the more magnetic memory you retain for biologic hysteresis. The more you retain UV light the more epigenetic control over genes to limit ubiquitin rates in protein replacements. Since our mitochondria has bacterial lineage we need to know how bacterial create hysteresis for our land of origin. In bacteria, this compaction is governed by the family of nucleoid-associated proteins (NAPs). Under conditions of stress and starvation, an NAP called Dps (DNA-binding protein from starved cells) becomes highly up-regulated and can massively reorganize the bacterial chromosome.

This likely is a big deal in shaping our haplotypes.

These small proteins that control hysteresis in mtDNA are like ferritin. Did you know that ferritin has a special protein coat that shield the iron crystals in the Fe (III) oxidation state? Did you know ferritin is PARAMAGNETIC? How might this be linked to its ability to cause disease? Any electromagnetic field causes molecules that are paramagnetic to be drawn towards that magnetic field. Could this be how ferritin is linked to many 21st century diseases? When iron cycles are not controlled in our blood plasma it can oxidize our mitochondria in tissues where this occurs. This lowers are redox power and can cause us to age faster. So when ferritin is high in your blood it is a sign to me that you are losing your magnetic hysteresis and the magnetic organization of the blue print of life in us........when we lose this the genie is out of the bottle and we need the wizard behind the curtain to help us regain our magnetic powers.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-23T18:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-228735>

High ferritin in blood plasma does.

Why do they? Because of an ignorance of light and how it works in us.

We recently had a new baby born into our family yesterday to nurse in our family. Question came up in her room and on the forum that was similar. So here is my PSA Q & A for you all today to understand how leptin light and ubiqutin marry.

"Long ago I said in leptin FAQ's babies get programmed via leptin/fat in breast milk...does/could high fat meal with no gall Bladder (deuterium collection) no bile supplied etc..... possibly amt to surge of leptin further exacerbating leptin insensitivity...inhibiting or slowing Leptin Rx? Answer : The leptin protein from mom has to act like a USB drive for the babies hypothalamus where is leptin receptor is awaiting instructions.

It contains and exRNA exosome with electron and proton inforamtion coded by light to excite bio-molecules like leptin and elovanoids in the exosome. The maternal egg gave the child its mitochondrial clone genome haplotype but that is only part of the quantum information transfer. That genome has to work with the nuclear DNA in the CURRENT ENVIRONMENT the child resides. What if the data in the exosome is not equivalent to the energy imprint in the environment? How does this effect the hysteresis imprint in RNA and DNA?

Breastfeeding is hwo the software is upgraded by the infant. Mom's deuterium content, electrons content, DHA content, her smells, sounds, and her plasma are all ways the baby gains information from Mom. So breastfeeding gives the child's mitochondria that information from his mother's leptin protein in her breastmilk. It needs the info of the precise environment it is in at birth. It gets this from momma's boob's assuming they are loaded with DHA. Women who have dry boobs usually are LR due to a serious lack of DHA or an excessive amount of protons with a 1 spin quantum number. The worse situation is if both are present. If momma is LR, the baby will adjust the nuclear mitochondrial distance for optimization by altering its Tensegrity system to get the right distance between mitochondrial DNA and nuclear DNA to optimized the match.

This goes on for the first 6 years of life when it appears the system is adaptable and learns before magnetic hysteresis locks things down. After that it appears it gets hard wired into the childs cells via RNA and DNA. Remember, the egg that the baby became is selected by leptin excitation of mom. If leptin was not coherently formed the information is transfered by electrons, protons, and light data in leptin. The childs leptin receptor si built to access that quantum upload. Leptin in women control fecundity. Have you ever wondered why women are born with over 1 million eggs but they can only use 400 in their life? In each egg is a mitochondrial clone from your ancestors pasts lives (immortaility in the stem cell line) that codes for a specific environment. When you ovulate a sperm climbs into your fallopian tube there is a complex interaction between its haploid number and the light it releases. That interaction of light and the atomic lattice selects the best egg for the current environment that mom senses.......If your environment is totally screwed.........you select no egg because the risk of mitonuclear de coupling is the worst out come or mismatch of energy utilization in biology. So you will be infertile. Now you see why fertility docs are printing money today........we live in a 24/7 blue lit microwaved world............and you have no eggs that match that situation. The egg has a plan......it wants to live in a child potential. If it senses that potential energy is not in the environment that egg will shrivel. Life preserves energy and light and will not manifest in a shit environment. The egg says: "Do not die while you’re still alive." An elevated ubiquitin rates tells the egg the information and won't allow that to happen. Oysters and AM sunlight are a kiss from the sea that prevents the kiss of death or illness visiting a child too early. Modern humans have even figured out how to get around this........and this is why we see things like brain stem glioma, ADHD, autism, cystic fibrosis, and progeria today in kids.

This song to me, feels like Levitation.... I witnessed it live last month, once in Copenhagen and then paid a £1000 to watch them again in Paris 2 weeks later because I needed to have that feeling one more time. They came out to it in Paris, and I actually felt like I was taking off...

### kris90 — 2017-11-23T22:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-228777>

_

We are searchlights, we can see in the dark
We are rockets, pointed up at the stars
We are billions of beautiful hearts
And you sold us down the river too far

What about us?
What about all the times you said you had the answers?
What about us?
What about all the broken happy ever afters?
What about us?
What about all the plans that ended in disaster?
What about love? What about trust?
What about us?

We are problems that want to be solved
We are children that need to be loved
We were willing, we came when you called
But man you fooled us, enough is enough

Sticks and stones they may break these bones
But then I'll be ready, are you ready?
It's the start of us, waking up, come on
Are you ready? I'll be ready
I don't want control, I want to let go
Are you ready? I'll be ready
Cause now it's time to let them know
We are ready
_
Perhaps words spoken from a mitochondriac? As humans we are supposed to be just as innocent as all living things on earth. We have amazing capabilities, we are beautiful and unique. We want to learn, want to love and be loved, we are willing to help, listen and comply, but we need to be guided appropriately. We trusted our higher powers for so long, and they manipulated us all. They made promises they could not keep, and failed us completely. But now we are waking up. We see the big picture, and we are ready.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-24T03:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-into-the-deep-and-dark.20437/post-228782>

Black swan's are a unique species, they are not a modern stereotype. We decided the buck stopped with us. Mitochondriacs are unstoppable because medicine has become the unusable to the layman. It has become highly profitable for corporation to to bind together researchers to focus their attention of study in the wrong direction than to follow the solo correct path science requires. This has cause man to suffer, while the scientists are benefiting, by having funding. This process has been going on now over 70 years, and has created the opportunity for black swans, in my opinion. This set of circumstances has created a unique situation on the internet. It is a place filled with noise of assertive idiots rather than the introspective wise black swans.
**The internet is noisy just make sure your message isn’t. Be clear of what you stand for**
Black swan's, in particular, are relentless for natural truths. We don't compromise grace. We use natural wisdom as our therapy.
But when we hunt natural cures, we're vicious in our purpose. I will never give up on what I love, the things where my passion lies. I will never back down. I will give no quarter. I will never let go of anything that is important to me. This is something few people know about me. Sometimes, when you are worn down by your job, your place in society, day after day, relentlessly, with no reprieve for years piled on years, sometimes you lose everything to give in.Make it a habit to give yourself enough reasons to relentlessly push forward with a high level of enthusiasm in your chosen craft. You are more mentally stronger than you could ever possibly imagine because of the struggles. Nature speaks to us with only her eyes. You speak for yourself with your actions. If a mitochondriac trips and falls, tomorrow is another opportunity to relentlessly and confidently push harder for your goals. You never give in until you're living in its reality. When you get that reality you are the black swan because you build arks for folks before it is even raining. They have no idea why you are doing what you're doing, but you keep doing it for the policy of truth.

# Thread: Sienna's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sienna — 2017-11-14T23:43:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-228014>

Hello everyone!

Really enthused about joining this community! Let’s get right into this journal!

Okay so I’ve been feeling much less than optimal for the past 3-4 years, I’ll try to keep my journey so far as brief as possible yet informative as I would love the community input! My main symptoms are as follows:

**\- Weight loss resistance (have gained about 15 kilograms and can’t get a kg off no matter what)

\- Rosacea (both redness and the bumps. Bumps have mostly disappeared since I’ve stopped wearing moisturiser or makeup, but still get them around ovulation period)

\- Extremely high AM cortisol (see lab screenshot below)

\- Hypothyroidism

\- Other skin problems: Lots of stretchmarks around hips and legs and arms (that appeared LONG AFTER the weight gain so no direct correlation) keratosis pilaris, dandruff.

\- Excessive belching
\- Low stomach acid

\- Fat malabsorption

\- Heart palpitations-- heart pounding and racing (particularly in the morning but still can get them throughout day)

\- Gut transit time: 4-5 days!**

Currently 22 years old. Pretty much my symptoms began approximately 3-6 months in 2014 after moving to Melbourne from the Gold Coast. I began to see a functional practitioner a year ago when symptoms refused to subside despite my efforts, and my GI stool test showed very elevated levels of good bacteria (80% of them were elevated) and fat malabsorption, which led him to suspect SIBO. No parasites or infections according to the test. Also tried AIP diet elimination after being paleo for 6 years, zero improvement. Also with a very thorough antimicrobial SIBO treatment (SO many supplements), I experienced zero improvements during and after treatment. Not even die-off symptoms, just feeling exactly the same. Very strange. I later transferred to a doctor who had CIRS training after it was suspected I had mould toxicity. I have at least one of the susceptible haplotypes (either 1-5, or 4-3-53, or both - Australian test isn’t has accurate as US) and for my doctor the labs suggested that I’ve had mould exposure in the past but seemed to be out of the mould for a while now (this still leads me to be skeptical of my diagnosis as I had only just left my mouldy house when I took the test, so no idea how my c4a wasn’t very high if it is indeed a biotoxin illness).

I did however have a MARCONS nasal infection. I’ve retested and apparently it’s gone (with such little improvement these days I wonder if it actually is sometimes.) So currently I’ve passed the VCS test and taken VIP for nearly four months now. I’m more than a little concerned though as I really don’t feel that different despite my “progress” through the treatment.

Here are some labs that I’ve taken:

**\- Leptin- 17 ng/ml**

**Biotoxin panel:**
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Liver function:**

**Adrenal Cortex:**

I have taken a lot more than these, like HS CRP (was normal apparently) and iron and thyroid panel (indicated possible hypothyroidism), however I do not remember nor have the numbers, will ask for them in my next appointment so I can post on here. Already been doing the leptin reset/ketogenic diet for at least three months prior so to have leptin borderline high still is concerning. Diet is clearly not enough for me!

Despite spending hundreds of dollars on supplements, adaptogens and diets, nothing has ever really worked, rather I have declined more since I began this health journey (eating poorly does aggravate everything however.) So I'm feeling more and more like the case in point that supplements and biochemical treatments are a waste of time because the change needs to occur in your environment.

So, my current thoughts are that when I moved to Melbourne I pretty much committed every sin that Jack is against: living in an apartment with EMFs everywhere, drinking fluoridated water, not being grounded, avoiding the sun etc. Prior to the move I considered myself to be in a healthy condition, had good control over my weight and skin was healthy so quite a stark contrast. I do go back to my family home often for up to 3 months at a time, and that place is rural/isolated, uses bore water, is right next to a pine forest, but perhaps I wasn’t doing enough to reverse the health issues (nothing like CT or avoiding blue light/wifi, and getting sun). For the meantime I’m doing the best I can with my current poor environment, but I am moving back there permanently in two weeks to intensely focus on water, light and magnetism. I have the luxury of being able to camp in a forest with no problems!

My current supplements that I’ve been taking for the past few months are:

\- Ox bile every meal (500 mg)

\- betaine HCL

\- DHEA (50mg), doctor’s prescription

\- Transdermal magnesium oil

**I’ve pretty much run out of ideas as to what my body wants, and I’m still pretty new to all this and very slowly going through the posts and blogs, so if I’ve missed some important labs or possible treatments that you think will help me finally make some progress/diagnosis please let me know! Feeling more than a bit unsure at the moment.
**
Oh and a bit of family info:

My mother I would say is a healthy woman compared to the rest her age and in her family, it doesn’t seem like she’ll be stopping anytime soon. She’s always outside and going to the beach and very active and diet is great. My grandmother (maternal) has rheumatoid arthritis after it was triggered by a virus, she was a little younger than what my mother is now at the time when it happened. My aunt has Hashimotos and has suffered with weight issues all her life, I don’t remember her being well ever. Other aunt is a smoker and underweight.

Thanks if you've read this far!

Hey Wendy, wonderful to meet you! Did you do the conventional and/or functional medicine route for treating SIBO or did you finally heal with Jack's recommendations? If not, what worked for you? I ask because I wouldn't be surprised if I tested positive for SIBO but not sure how much a positive result would help anything, as I'm hesitant re-treating and spending another $1500 on supplements until I'm given reason to believe it would actually work this time round? ### Sienna — 2017-11-30T09:26:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-229180>

Landed on the Gold Coast, and I went to my doctor appointment today and he feels I am ready to do HCG, provided I take natural desiccated thyroid as it is not doing great:

**TSH: 4.52 mU/L (ref range: 0.5-5.0)**
FT4: 10.8 pmol/L
**FT3: 3.5 pmol/L (ref range 2.6-60)**
TgAb: <10 IU/mL
TPOAb: 13 IU/mL

I'm hoping however very soon I won't need the thyroid meds anymore. I am hopeful for the next few months as I am no longer drinking fluoride and increasing my exposure to sunlight, DHA etc. I also have an infrared light bulb I can shine on my thyroid. I am detoxing the fluoride with zeolite (whether that is necessary or not with iodine, I'm not sure, but just in case). Also an issue was my testosterone levels:

**Testosterone: 2.8 nmol/L** (ref range: <1.8)
SHBG: 87 nmol/L (25-150 range)
Free Testosterone: 27 pmol/L (1-34)
DHEA: 6.2 umol/L (4.0-11.7)

He thinks possible PCOS, which numerous health gurus have suggested over the years, but since I don't have consistent symptoms and the labs for it, it has remained unconfirmed. DHEA has increased since last time so he's switching me to 7 KETO DHEA 75mg for a remaining three months. Another thing he was slightly puzzled by was my white blood cells and he's not sure why they are low (I'm assuming it will all lead back to light, water and magnetism somehow):

White Cells: 3.4 (4.0-11.0)
Neutrophils: 1.7 (2.0-7.5)

Due to menstrual timing and xmas holidays, I've decided to wait till January to start the HCG. Speaking of menstruation, one thing I'm interested to see if my menstrual cramps flare up while I'm back here. Strangely, back in Melbourne I would have minimal cramps, however here I have such painful cramps that I want to vomit and pass out. So strange, I don't have much to base it on- maybe increased carb intake? My doctor reckons it was the mould, probably makes sense and so far I don't have a great alternate theory. So I'm hoping it doesn't come back otherwise I will be concerned as to what is wrong or if I've re-exposed myself to mould. From my reading on here so far, I'm getting the impression and sincerely hope, that by following the mitochondriac way, that mould won't be a concern for me anymore.

We shall also see how my belching and remaining rosacea goes, he wants to test for SIBO if they don't go down during the HCG or thyroid meds. Very early to say, but my heart palpitations were fantastic this morning aka. hardly existent. We shall see. And I'm still planning a camp out when the weather clears up.

I also have the cool fat burner (and the gut buster) and going to try combining it with time in my pool (perhaps putting my lower half in while wearing the vests) Don't think I can afford constantly filling the bathtub with ice cubes nor do I have the room for it. Not sure if ice packs from the cool fat burner would be good enough at reducing the temps (got about 12, so let me know if you strongly disagree). Gonna be some troubleshooting happening for the heat of summer.

The ground feels different here I've noticed. Perhaps placebo thinking, but it actually feels amazing putting my feet on the ground compared to the Melbourne soil. Praying with all my heart, that this new start is the beginning of my healing.

### Sienna — 2017-12-04T07:51:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-229449>

I had time to properly go through my test results today and noticed that my doctor did test for urea and creatinine in a multiple biochemical analysis, very happy as I thought I'd have to go back and request them. He did not make any comments on it because it was within the ranges and nor is he looking at what Jack is.
**Urea was 5.0 mmol/L
Creatinine was 72 umol/L**

I put it through an online BUN/CREAT calculator and it came to **17.2 ratio** (hopefully that is accurate). Honestly not as bad as I thought it would be, still bad of course, but thought it would be higher. So if it needs to be around 10:1, I have some work to do, luckily this was taken over a month ago in Melbourne with, as I have said, a very poor environment. So fingers crossed my new environment improves it quickly before the HCG. And I've seen people on here half theirs so I'm hopeful I can do it. Get in that DHA stat!

I've had menstrual cramps for the past couple of days, probably 50% of the worst they've been, so pretty bearable, but annoying to figure out why its happening again. But I have a suspicion that it is not mould but it is the carbs, somehow. During my move I had carbs for the first time in a while. Definitely wasn't a lot, but with the timing with my cycle it could've set me off. And don't know how I forgot, but I remember that I was feeling the starting of cramps before I even got home. So mould seems to be diminishing in importance, would be great if mould has very little to do on my path to wellness, even though I've spent money and time on it, it has in a way brought me to this site so I'd be grateful for that reason even if it's a misdiagnosis. Would make my life easier in not having to avoid mould obsessively as well. I wonder though if it's possible to get Marcons nasal infection even if you aren't mould-toxic? (my MSH was low-normal and I remember that if your MSH is low you are more susceptible to Marcons).

Would like to get prog/E2 measured soon, I think it was tested at the beginning of the year however I do not have that test nor would I consider it very reliable anymore due to the changes over the year.

Am also going to buy some methylene blue and see how I go on it.

Hey Caroline! I'm technically on the NSW side, closer to Coolangatta but inner west in the valleys, nice little hub away from the city. ### Sienna — 2017-12-06T01:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-229598>

Rosacea has been making slow improvements, today is I think the best day it has been in a loooong time. The more sun it gets the better, which totally contradicts the dogma of rosacea, and I know many that are convinced their rosacea gets worse with sun, so I avoided too it for years. Still patches of dryness, but I remember that was one of the first symptoms I had so it will probably be the last to go. If it keeps up, it may be completely resolved in 3 months!

### Sienna — 2017-12-18T12:19:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-230541>

My mind is completely calm upon waking and I'll meditate early in the morning in the sun, but around 7-8am I can feel my heart begin to feel stressed. It's concerning considering it's possibly still high AM cortisol, but I'll give it more time and if still no improvement I'll do more digging and re-evaluate (always am). I'm interested to see if there is a marked improvement when I camp for at least 4 days soon. It's frustrating and sometimes I sulk and think I haven't made any progress in the last 3-4 years and will never heal but I then remember things that have resolved on my journey such as my post-dinner facial flushing (it only happened in the evening if I had carbs, carbs earlier in the day was fine but for some reason nighttime was not good) and my 1-3 am wake ups, even though they aren't the ones that I've been so focused on resolving. And in a way, I feel I have only just gotten started.

In regards to sleep, it has been weird. Ever since I began the ketogenic diet months ago my body has a difficult time most days sleeping past 7am, today was more unusual, I woke up at 5:20 am and was wide awake and couldn't fall back asleep despite not getting 8 hours (instead approx. 7 hours). Went outside to watch the sunrise. The sun is coming up earlier and earlier and my window is quite large so light does come in, so maybe I'm attuning to that? That and the laughing kookaburras I think does it. Cause of my early starts I'm getting tired earlier and wanting to be in bed by 9:30pm.

I've been taking the remainder of my compounded cholestyramine for the past few days as it's nearly expired, was not cheap and I wanted to see if I felt any better on it in a new house. Again, I wonder if I just had a msh deficiency from avoiding sunlight (which led to marcons and everything else) and not mould toxicity, cause I truly notice very little difference in how I feel; I have gone through the entirety of the shoemaker protocol and I can't credit any resolutions of symptoms to it despite my "progress" through it. After Jack's Q&A today I feel more assured I'm not going to get far by focusing obsessively on mould avoidance or detoxing, especially considering my biomarkers weren't substantially elevated and neither of the strategies have given me any strong indication that they help. I'm convinced that it all went downhill because I moved to a poor environment in Melbourne, so now I just need to put the effort into biohacking that will improve my redox potential.

My methylene blue finally arrived, started with one drop (o.5 mg) and see how I feel at first for a few days and up it if I feel it necessary. A couple of hours after taking it I noticed that my mind was very switched on, to the point of restlessness if I'm being idle, I feel the need to read and occupy my mind or get up and do some physical activity. At the moment it borders on being slightly uncomfortable, I can feel my heart pumping a bit more than usual- might have to lower dose. I should note I have no idea if this is the MB's doing or something else entirely. Will try again at different times to see if I get similar effects again.

The CFB has been fine so far, though I really feel like I could do it more intensely, it doesn't feel as effective compared to being in icy water, but it has been difficult to increase the intensity with it in summer. Going to try and get an empty icecream container to freeze an ice block to put in the bath tub and see if it gets it suitably cold. If that works out, will still use CFB but just on days when I'm waiting for water to freeze.

Yeah upon being on the mould avoidance facebook groups for a little while I noticed a trend quickly emerge that so many people were struggling and just being so negative about their lack of progress. Common post was "I moved into a new house with no mould and I still feel awful!" (even with shoemaker protocol). They are constantly scrambling to find the source of mould, whereas it sounds like to me like an EMF issue, as more often than not they are moving into apartments. Plus I know someone who had completely recovered, and yet have a minor relapse for "unknown" reasons, she has not been able to find the source of the mould nor are binders helping her that much the second time round. Avoiding mould seems like such a difficult task, it's absolutely everywhere and avoiding it for life doesn't seem like an optimal solution. I get it with nnEMFs, but mould always made scratch my head a bit. Also astounded that sunlight rarely, if ever, gets discussed by mould experts, when it seems so tied to everything. I would read mould/lyme conference notes thoroughly and I have yet to see anyone mention a single thing about light.

Anyway, thanks for the calculator link! Will have a go and report back soon how my ice bath was!

### Sienna — 2017-12-27T13:02:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-231135>

I've been reading more and more on DDW recently and trying to figure out if my underground spring water is high in deuterium. My water comes from deep underground, apparently from under a rock bed. I would consider sending a sample to ANSTO like Richelle has, if they are obliging. I've also read a possible indication of high deuterium could be in the appearance of the ice e.g. a lot of white, not very transparent? For now, I've bought Icelandic glacial water from Woolies till I get a better idea of what's going on. Been going for walks on the beach in the morning, the sun is really hot these days.

MB has had some positive effects thus far, and I've shared it with my family. Both mother and I have reported significantly better sleep, and father with reduced neurological issues. Unlike most supplements I've taken in the past, this one actually feels like it makes some tangible difference for me and my family. I'm happy that my sleep seems to have improved. Small improvement, but hopefully those gains turn into bigger ones. Not expecting it to be a miracle worker, but I'll take anything that helps improves my redox potential. For Christmas Mum bought me 4 raw hematite rocks as I read on a forum on here that they would be helpful in mitigating EMF exposure. They are too large and heavy to keep in my bag or pocket, so I put them at the four corners of my bed.

I've noticed that my keto rash (well whatever it was, I can only correlate it to when I began a ketogenic diet), has been gone for a long time leading me to wonder if I am in ketosis or not, or that my body has removed the toxins. Surely I'd be in ketosis as my carb intake is very low. It wasn't a severe keto rash by any means, only a small patch on my chest.

My rosacea has halted in improvement at the moment, sometimes I'm not sure if it is improving or staying more or less the same. My face is getting tanner, so maybe it's just my skin colour is helping conceal the redness somewhat. And as usual, around ovulation I've been getting a few pustules. Belching is also more or less the same. I'm really trying to crack that one, feels like as long as I am belching there is still something not quite right with my gut health. No idea if my AM cortisol level has improved yet either.

It's been hard to further reduce EMF exposure in my household. I've made positive changes and I know more could be done, but it's difficult without the complete support of my family, I'm one against four, all of which have no signficant health issues and therefore do not perceive EMFs as a significant issue. Undoubtedly they know EMFs aren't great, but they aren't convinced of their damaging extent enough to go to the lengths I will go. I'm desperate for the reversal of my issues, they however are not. I'm a deep lover of Japanese culture and I'm aware of how low their obesity rates are and yet what an EMF toxic environement they live in and how high their carb intake is. Prior to Jack I have gone "Whaaa?? What kind of genes do they have??" Is it the DHA in the marine based diet? Something in their water? The magnetic fields of their earth? I'd be happy to eat as much seafood as I can if it has that much of an effect in mitigating the consequences of EMFs.

### Sienna — 2018-05-01T06:06:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/siennas-optimal-journal.20438/post-240271>

It's been a while since I've posted. Took some time to focus on me and dig more. Long story short, I have chronic tonsil and mouth infections. I went to a holistic dentist after doing a thermography that found my throat and mouth showing heavy signs of inflammation. I didn't tell the dentist about the thermography at first as I have found in my case it led the witness so to speak, but the dentist took one look at my mouth and tonsils and said I had a chronic infection, likely the parasite _entamoeba gingivalis._ He further confirmed with an electroacupuncture biofeedback testing that my tonsils, along with my spleen, have really high inflammation readings. Usually I take such readings with a grain of salt but to have two completely different tests point to the tonsils, I think that there may be something to it. The other organ readings also highly corresponded to symptomatic problem areas e.g. thyroid, heart, lymph nodes etc. So I think it's a pretty fascinating device.

I also learnt that amoebas destroy WBCs, which would explain why my blood work showed my neutrophils as quite low.

I've been red lighting inside my mouth for months now even prior to my dentist appointment, so I'm not sure how hard I'm going to have to go to get rid of this infection if redlight is not making a huge difference already. Have also stained my mouth with MB and exposed it to light for photodynamic therapy ( I think it should help?). I'm currently doing a protocol by Dr. Mark Bonner that uses hydrogen peroxide and a combination of bi-carb + salt. I'm nearly two weeks into that but I suspect I'll be doing it for another 2 1/2 months at least. However I'm not sure what to do about the tonsils specifically, I've been struggling to find info on the internet about dealing with this. They do look quite sad- pale with small white specks (possibly tonsils stones, but too small for me to get out by myself). People like Klinghardt suggest ozone/neural therapy, and if that doesn't work cryotherapy directly on the tonsils (treatment only in Germany) and if that's doesn't work or not possible, surgical removal. I really don't see myself getting surgery but the government has cracked down on ozone therapy here so it's hard to find a dentist that does it nearby. I'll keep looking for ozone therapy in the meantime, but I'm thinking of painting them with iodine to start with.

I have been diligently following Jack's basic rules for 6 months now so I'm concerned about what I need to do further to nip this in the butt. Only aspect I could do better on is the cold ice baths. I haven't been able to do it everyday or even weekly sometimes as I don't have cheap or free access to icy cold water. Winter is coming though so I suspect the frequency will pick up soon.

Is there something I could ingest or swish in my mouth that according to quantum biology would be effective in dealing with tonsil infections? Or is there an aspect of Jack's protocols that I should focus more intensely on?I still have my eye on 25ppm DDW, however I don't want to spend over $1200 for ONE month's supply if that is not _the_ thing I need to get right now to heal my mouth and tonsils.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-15T04:45:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-for-deuterium-depletion.20439/post-228033>

Most fructans are high in deuterium. Most food guru's do not realize they can cause gluten sensitivity. Celiac sensitivity is not just a gluten problem, it is a damn deuterium problem. All those paleo AI protocols go BOOM. Some of those paleo guru's are pulling over 3 million a year on utter bullshit. A fructan is a polymer of fructose molecules. Fructans with a short chain length are known as fructooligosaccharides. Fructans occur in foods such as agave, artichokes, asparagus, leeks, garlic, onions, yacón, jícama, and wheat. Interestingly, rice grasses cannot make fructans so make not of why Asian cuisine side steps a lot of issues because of the mix of DHA and rice.
Fructans also appear in grass (C3), with dietary implications for horses and other grazing animals. So believe it or not grass can cause cows a lot of fructan issues that mimic colic.
Fructans may be associated with symptoms attributed to gluten-sensitivity by self-reporting non-celiac patients. [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0016508517363023](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0016508517363023)

From my time on my parents farm.

Cows eat fresh green grass and drink water without any problems.
Horses are fed with fresh green clover.
Cows are not to be given fresh green clovers, but they love to eat it.
If cow breaks in and eat green clover she must be restrained from drinking water.
If that kind of cow drinks water, it builds up gas and usually die unless her stomach is punctured to relieve gas pressure.

### Brent Patrick — 2019-02-07T00:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-for-deuterium-depletion.20439/post-262443>

For any mushroom product to be of benefit the process has to be dual extract so both water and alcohol constituents of the mushroom are extracted.
You then have a medicinal substance that has both Triterpenes and beta-glucans, most mushrooms products on the market lack this important process.

Highly recommended reading

[https://www.amazon.com/Medicinal-Mushrooms-Exploration-Tradition-Healing/dp/1570671435](https://www.amazon.com/Medicinal-Mushrooms-Exploration-Tradition-Healing/dp/1570671435)

[https://www.amazon.com/Mycelium-Running-Mushrooms-Help-World/dp/1580085792/ref=sr\_1\_10?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1549492621&sr=1-10&keywords=Medicinal+Mushrooms:](https://www.amazon.com/Mycelium-Running-Mushrooms-Help-World/dp/1580085792/ref=sr_1_10?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1549492621&sr=1-10&keywords=Medicinal+Mushrooms%3A)

But knowing when, what or not to take is based on your geographical location and far more tricky
then just consuming mushroom products from across the planet willy nilly

[@Sun Disciple](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19565/) I've used the Host Defense product for 5 years and thought it was working well. However after listening to the Luke Storey podcast, I agree with the idea proposed by Jeff at Real Mushrooms. The percentage of mushroom actually in the mycelium products would have to be lower, since the mycelium root is hard to remove the from the brown rice and thus the product is mostly likely more starch. So even if more mycelium is good or not, Host Defense does not state any percentage of mycelium to starch content on their website or product packaging. I just dont know how much mushroom is in the product. If I am paying $30 a bottle, I would like more mushrooms.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-15T17:16:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-turmeric-quantum-rx-for-health.20442/post-228053>

Why is tumeric and DHA a natural pair?

They are the scaffold for how the retina is built for sunlight.

Developing an action spectrum is a fundamental means evolution used for determining the input physiology for the circadian system. This is why DHA and tumeric are ideal pairs to build the retina that connects to the SCN and pituitary and to the pineal gland by way of the blood plasma and CSF pathways. This photobiological technique has high utility for
(1) defining the relative effectiveness of photons at different wavelengths for eliciting a biological response
(2) identifying the specific pho- topigment involved in that response (Lipson, 1994; Coohill, 1999). The specific aim of the present study was to characterize the wavelength sensitivity of the photoreceptor system responsible for providing circadian input to the human pineal gland by establishing an action spectrum for light-induced melatonin suppression. The experiments defined an action spectrum that fits a retinaldehyde opsin template and identified 446–477 nm as the most potent wavelength region for regulating melatonin is melanopsin. Univariance among the eight fluence–response curves suggests that a single photopigment is primarily responsible for melatonin suppression in humans. It turns out melanopsin is that photopigment and how it works in us is very counterintuitive. It terminates the effect of melatonin in the eye and this opens the flood gates for melatonin to work in the pinela gland when it is DARK at night. They key is understanding how melatonin and melanopsin are made by AM sunlight. The are many results now published that have suggested and proven that melanopsin is the novel photopigment in the human eye that mediates circadian photoreception. I believe melanopsin and crytptochromes working in the retina are also where human magneto-reception is based. As redox in the retina drops, we lose our magneto-sense. This is why modern humans appear to no thave it. We do.......you just have to look for it. This sense in the retina works with our hair follicles which act as antennas for the reception. Your eyes and hair are linked in ways you cannot imagine. DHA and curcumin help build this quantum network.

### Sean Waters — 2017-11-17T14:42:05+0200

It's interesting - after initial diagnosis of Crohn's in 2015 I started taking Omega 3 supps and taking Curcumin at doses btween 3000mg to 6000mg.

I was able to come off all medication in the space of a few months and never looked back. I always tell people that Curcumin reversed my disease, and that CT and Sun keep it absent from my life. Curcumin at 3 - 6g dose is expensive though, I spent £200 a month on it.

Since then, I've stopped using supplements for CT, Sunlight and actual food DHA. But I think I'm going to start adding back heavy amounts of Turmeric into my DHA meals to reap this benefit once again....

So apparently turmeric contains roughly 3% curcumin. Each teaspoon = 0.1 to 0.2 grams of curcumin.

To get the doses I required in my inflammatory state, you would need 15 to 30 teaspoons of turmeric a day... Your blood would be dyed orange, absorbing all that extra UV though. I used Good Health Naturally curcumin, but there are loads out there. These days I throw about 4 or 5 teaspoons into a big shrimp and fish curry when I make it.

Indians rub this stuff on their skin in festivals... Wonder would it help the skin as a UV fluorophore?

I'm spending Christmas in India this year... Seafood, curry, sun and probably too many beers for 4 weeks. Cannot wait.

### drezy — 2017-11-19T17:32:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-turmeric-quantum-rx-for-health.20442/post-228413>

Wow your parties sounded way more interesting than any I've ever been invited to!

Even worse, I think it all starts with some crap mitochondria you could get passed to you. That's not even a choice.

Mmmmmmm, vindaloo!
Fast fact -- Food History: Vindaloo was a collaborative effort to meet the needs of Portuguese sailors. Those dudes wanted meat for the trip home. The real ingenious part of the dish is the design. When made in the original manner the vindaloo ends up not only in vinegar but it's swimming in pork fat. If you pour it into a cylindrical container, like the sailors did, you'd end up with a perfect solidified fat cap on the top that prevented aerobic bacteria and spoilage. I made it this way once and I'm never going to make it any other way from now on.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-20T03:08:01+0200

DHA needs water in the retina to work in nonlinear fashion.

Life is based upon a hydrogen economy because a mitochondria is really a hydrogen furnace. This hydrogen must not be predominantly deuterium. It powers all things that animate atoms to scale the thermodynamic ledge from abiotic to biotic. What is the thermodynamic key of this change in behavior. The more solar light energy that is pumped into the system and not thermalized allows the cells to become further from equilibrium. The further from equilibrium we go the more likely life manifests because the organization inside the cell becomes like an exotic condensed matter state called a Bose-Einstein condensate. Now you have a short answer from me, What is Life. This phase transition of starlight energy by water in a cell is what makes a life. Starlight is what changes the pH in water that surrounds all proteins. Proteins form the the analogue semiconductive portion of life that acts in linear fashion. The addition of water around proteins is what allowed the nonlinear process to dominate the biochemical pathways. This is the digital system in the cell. As quantum mechanics endows particles with surprising properties, it enables to explain physical processes like electrolysis. Starlight is capable of separating water into its charges while also building a liquid crystalline array inside a cell to allow for non linear optics using light in ways biology still cannot comprehend. While thermodynamics gives a consistent account of them, independent of any mechanism, quantum mechanics provides a consistent explanation of electron fluxes crossing the interface between a metallic conductor and an aqueous environment. Cells are filled with specific metallic ions suspended in many types of water that cell is expert in making. What type of water is made is wholly dependent upon the redox power in the cell at that particular moment based upon the physics present in the environment. This redox power is wholly tied to the environment and the frequencies of starlight that is present and stored in water networks around our bio-molecules that make electric power from electric and magnetic fields in sunlight. Understanding how electron travels is important in many fields: hydrogen production, deuterium depletion, environmental scanning tunneling microscopy, scanning electrochemical microscopy and bio-sensing applications. It also will turn out in the coming age of quantum biology to be the single biggest insight of mankind in my opinion. [https://phys.org/news/2017-11-quantum-tunnelling-biosensing.html](https://phys.org/news/2017-11-quantum-tunnelling-biosensing.html)

Wisdom and knowledge are not synonyms. Those who seek wisdom must seek many truths to gain insight. They must be tenacious. Knowledge is a part of ignorance that man arranges and classify. Knowledge has the shelf life of fish in a market. It is not sustainable and does not always lead to truth. If knowledge is information, wisdom is the understanding and application of that knowledge and insight is the awareness of the underlying essence of a truth.
Sadly we can gain a lifetime of knowledge, yet never see the wisdom in it. We can be wise but still miss the deeper meaning of facts. Today medicine is a good example of this. There are many who know things among us but lacking in wisdom. Your mentors should be filled with wisdom because they will be the connectors of life. Their insight will be indefatigable for you.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-15T19:41:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-rx-and-the-exosome.20444/post-228079>

This article links directly to my recently released October 2017 webinar on stem cells and oncognesis. The bio-physics of the exosome is not well known yet in medical circles, but it is now clear that the immune cells in marrow and in our blood are one of the foundational steps in stem cell activation and depletion in disease associated with mitochondrial dysfunction. Mitophagy (autophagy) is a ubiquitous catabolic process in living systems, which causes cellular bulk degradation of cytoplasmic components (mitochondria) and thereby regulates cellular homeostasis through the formation of these exosomes. They travel in the blood and lymph circulation to affect other tissues energy balance. The inactivation of autophagy has been linked with many detrimental effects to cells and organisms in the literature and now the picture is becoming clear how the immune system really operates within our tissues. What is the mitochondriac viewpoint here? ??? It appears from the data that mitophagy switches from advantageous to harmful in the context of an age-associated dysfunction because of the heteroplasmy rate of immune cells. As it rises the cellular response is not the same as we see in tissues with lower heteroplasmy states. [https://eurekalert.org/pub\_releases/2017-10/ibri-nkr092817.php](https://eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2017-10/ibri-nkr092817.php)
